description: 50 medium-to-hard questions covering AI concepts, tools, applications, and strategy.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the difference between supervised and unsupervised learning?

options.A: Supervised learning uses more GPUs than unsupervised learning
options.B: Supervised learning is only used for image recognition tasks
options.C: Supervised learning uses labeled data; unsupervised learning finds patterns in unlabeled data
options.D: Supervised learning works without any data; unsupervised learning requires large datasets

questions.question: What is 'hallucination' in the context of Large Language Models (LLMs)?

options.A: When the model processes images instead of text
options.B: When the model generates confident but factually incorrect or fabricated information
options.C: When the model takes too long to respond due to server overload
options.D: When the model refuses to answer sensitive questions

questions.question: In prompt engineering, what does 'few-shot prompting' mean?

options.A: Using short prompts to reduce token costs
options.B: Providing the model with a small number of input-output examples before your actual query
options.C: Limiting the number of API calls made per minute
options.D: Running the model on low-powered hardware with minimal resources

questions.question: Which metric is most appropriate to evaluate a classification model when the dataset is highly imbalanced?

options.A: F1 Score
options.B: R-squared
options.C: Mean Squared Error
options.D: Overall Accuracy

questions.question: What is Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) primarily used for?

options.A: Enhancing LLM responses by fetching relevant external knowledge at inference time
options.B: Generating synthetic training datasets from scratch
options.C: Training a brand new AI model on private company data
options.D: Reducing the physical size of neural network hardware

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a real application of AI in marketing?

options.A: AI-generated ad copy using language models
options.B: Predictive customer churn analysis
options.C: Personalized product recommendations
options.D: Automatic physical manufacturing of marketing banners

questions.question: What does 'fine-tuning' an AI model mean?

options.A: Writing better prompts to improve model output quality
options.B: Adjusting hardware settings for better GPU performance
options.C: Re-training a pre-trained model on a smaller domain-specific dataset
options.D: Deleting irrelevant layers from an existing neural network

questions.question: Which cloud provider offers the AI and ML service called 'SageMaker'?

options.A: Amazon Web Services (AWS)
options.B: Google Cloud Platform
options.C: Microsoft Azure
options.D: IBM Cloud

questions.question: In the context of AI automation, what is an 'AI Agent'?

options.A: A hardware component that speeds up AI computations
options.B: A customer service representative trained in using AI tools
options.C: An AI system that can autonomously plan and execute multi-step tasks using external tools
options.D: A chatbot that only handles predefined FAQ responses

questions.question: What is the primary role of the 'transformer' architecture in modern AI models?

options.A: It converts images to text automatically in real time
options.B: It uses self-attention mechanisms to understand relationships across all parts of input data simultaneously
options.C: It physically transforms hardware to process data faster
options.D: It transforms structured data into unstructured data for analysis

questions.question: Which of the following best defines 'AI bias'?

options.A: The time delay in AI model responses under heavy load
options.B: The preference of AI models for numerical data over text data
options.C: Systematic errors in AI outputs caused by flawed or unrepresentative training data
options.D: The tendency of AI models to prefer certain programming languages

questions.question: Why is a GPU critical for deep learning model training?

options.A: GPUs connect AI models directly to the internet for faster data fetching
options.B: GPUs have parallel processing architecture that accelerates matrix computations used in deep learning
options.C: GPUs handle all general-purpose computer operations efficiently
options.D: GPUs write AI code automatically without human input

questions.question: Which of the following is the best example of 'Generative AI'?

options.A: A spam filter that classifies incoming emails as spam or not spam
options.B: DALL-E generating original images from text descriptions
options.C: A fraud detection system that flags suspicious transactions
options.D: A recommendation engine that ranks products by purchase history

questions.question: What is 'transfer learning' in AI?

options.A: Applying knowledge from training on one task to improve performance on a different but related task
options.B: Moving an AI model from one cloud server to another
options.C: Converting a model from one programming language to another
options.D: Transferring user data between two separate AI systems

questions.question: In data analytics, what does 'data normalization' primarily achieve?

options.A: It organizes all data records into alphabetical order
options.B: It permanently removes all outliers from the dataset
options.C: It converts all data types to integer format for processing
options.D: It scales features to a standard range to prevent any single variable from dominating model training

questions.question: What is the key distinction between Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning?

options.A: AI is outdated technology that ML has completely replaced
options.B: AI is only for robotics applications; ML is exclusively for software
options.C: AI requires constant human supervision; ML operates fully autonomously
options.D: AI is a broader field of building intelligent systems; ML is a specific subset where systems learn patterns from data

questions.question: Which Python framework is most widely used for building and training deep learning models?

options.A: PyTorch or TensorFlow
options.B: Matplotlib
options.C: NumPy
options.D: Pandas

questions.question: What is 'overfitting' in a machine learning model?

options.A: When the model uses too many GPUs and consumes excessive energy
options.B: When the model performs well on training data but poorly on new, unseen data
options.C: When the model is too simple and fails to capture any meaningful patterns
options.D: When the model takes too long to complete its training process

questions.question: Which of the following is a well-known current limitation of AI language models?

options.A: Translating content between major languages
options.B: Writing grammatically correct and coherent sentences
options.C: Reliably performing complex real-time mathematical calculations without errors
options.D: Summarizing long documents into shorter paragraphs

questions.question: What does 'NLP' stand for and what is its primary focus in AI?

options.A: Natural Language Processing; enabling machines to understand and generate human language
options.B: Numeric Logic Programming; focused on mathematical computation tasks
options.C: Network Latency Protocol; focused on reducing AI response time
options.D: Neural Learning Protocol; focused on hardware optimization for AI

questions.question: In building an AI strategy for a business, what should be the FIRST step?

options.A: Hire a full team of data scientists and ML engineers immediately
options.B: Purchase the most advanced AI software licence available in the market
options.C: Build a fully custom AI model from scratch using internal data
options.D: Identify and clearly define the specific business problem AI is meant to solve

questions.question: What is 'Computer Vision' in AI?

options.A: Software that enhances and improves monitor display quality
options.B: A field of AI that enables machines to interpret and understand visual data such as images and video
options.C: A tool used to monitor computer network traffic visually
options.D: The ability of computers to display high-resolution 3D graphics in real time

questions.question: Which of the following is a valid and accurate use case of AI in cybersecurity?

options.A: Using physical AI robots to guard server rooms
options.B: Manually reviewing each network log with AI assistance
options.C: Using AI models to detect anomalous network behavior and predict cyber threats in real time
options.D: Replacing all human security professionals with fully automated AI systems

questions.question: What does 'tokenization' mean in the context of Large Language Models?

options.A: Awarding digital certificates and badges to trained AI models
options.B: Converting AI output into financial tokens for blockchain transactions
options.C: Encrypting user data before it is sent to the AI model for privacy
options.D: Splitting input text into smaller units called tokens that the model can process numerically

questions.question: What is the main purpose of an 'AI Readiness Assessment' for an organization?

options.A: To check whether the company's internet connection is fast enough for AI tools
options.B: To measure how quickly employees can learn to code AI models
options.C: To test whether the company's existing computers can run AI software
options.D: To evaluate the organization's data, skills, culture, and infrastructure before adopting AI at scale

questions.question: Which of the following best describes 'Reinforcement Learning'?

options.A: Learning by copying the exact behavior patterns of human domain experts
options.B: Learning by finding hidden clusters and patterns in completely unlabeled data
options.C: Learning through trial and error using rewards and penalties for actions taken
options.D: Learning exclusively from large datasets of pre-labeled training examples

questions.question: What is 'MLOps' in the AI industry?

options.A: A type of neural network architecture used in image processing
options.B: A marketing and sales strategy for AI-based products
options.C: A set of practices combining ML development and IT operations to reliably deploy and maintain models in production
options.D: A programming language specifically designed for writing ML algorithms

questions.question: Which of the following is the most significant challenge in deploying AI across rural India?

options.A: AI models not being powerful enough to handle rural use cases
options.B: Limited digital infrastructure, low AI literacy, and language diversity barriers
options.C: Excess availability of high-speed internet making AI too accessible in rural areas
options.D: An overabundance of AI-trained professionals avoiding urban areas

questions.question: What is 'Explainable AI' (XAI)?

options.A: A simplified beginner-friendly version of AI tools and platforms
options.B: AI techniques designed to make model decisions transparent and understandable to humans
options.C: AI systems that can explain jokes, humor, and cultural references to users
options.D: An AI tool that automatically generates technical documentation for code

questions.question: In AI product development, what is a 'Minimum Viable AI Product' (MVAP)?

options.A: The cheapest AI product currently available in the consumer market
options.B: An AI model trained using the minimum possible amount of data
options.C: A product that uses AI only for minor, non-critical background tasks
options.D: An AI-powered product with the minimum features needed to test core assumptions and collect user feedback

questions.question: Which tool is specifically designed for AI-powered workflow automation without writing code?

options.A: Docker
options.B: TensorFlow
options.C: Jupyter Notebook
options.D: Zapier with AI integrations or Make (formerly Integromat)

questions.question: What is the key difference between 'AI automation' and 'AI augmentation'?

options.A: Automation uses deep learning only; augmentation uses rule-based systems only
options.B: Automation is used only in manufacturing; augmentation is used only in software
options.C: Automation completely removes human tasks; augmentation enhances human capabilities with AI assistance
options.D: Automation is expensive and complex; augmentation is always free and simple

questions.question: What does 'data privacy' mean specifically in the context of AI systems?

options.A: Making all AI models fully open source and publicly available
options.B: Keeping AI model architecture and weights secret from business competitors
options.C: Ensuring personal data used to train or operate AI is collected, stored, and used ethically and legally
options.D: Restricting access to all AI tools to prevent any potential misuse

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the 'context window' of a Large Language Model?

options.A: The graphical user interface that appears when the model is actively running
options.B: The full version history of all the model's software updates
options.C: The time period during which the model was originally trained on data
options.D: The maximum amount of text (in tokens) the model can process and retain in a single interaction

questions.question: For a business leader evaluating an AI tool, which factor is MOST critical for measuring ROI?

options.A: Whether the tool is built on the most advanced and latest AI model available
options.B: The tool's ability to reduce measurable costs or generate quantifiable revenue relative to its implementation cost
options.C: The quality and attractiveness of the tool's user interface design
options.D: The AI vendor company's social media following and brand reputation

questions.question: What is 'multimodal AI'?

options.A: AI that can only be accessed through multiple different devices simultaneously
options.B: AI that supports communication in multiple human spoken languages
options.C: AI that uses multiple data center servers to distribute processing load
options.D: AI that can process and generate multiple types of data such as text, images, audio, and video

questions.question: Which foundational skill is most important before advancing to building custom AI models?

options.A: Advanced cloud computing infrastructure and server management
options.B: Expertise in semiconductor and hardware engineering
options.C: Proficiency in graphic design and data visualization aesthetics
options.D: Understanding and applying statistics and data analysis

questions.question: What is 'vector embedding' in the context of AI and NLP?

options.A: Representing words, sentences, or data as numerical vectors that capture semantic meaning
options.B: A security encryption method specifically designed for AI model weights
options.C: Embedding AI processing chips into consumer physical products
options.D: A method of compressing large video files using AI algorithms

questions.question: Which statement about AI in government services is most accurate?

options.A: AI is exclusively useful in government only for national defense applications
options.B: AI cannot be used in government due to strict data privacy and security regulations
options.C: AI can improve citizen services through chatbots and fraud detection while requiring strong governance frameworks
options.D: AI can replace all government officials to dramatically reduce operational costs

questions.question: What does 'model drift' mean in a deployed AI system?

options.A: The model gradually becoming faster over time due to incremental hardware upgrades
options.B: The model physically migrating to a different geographical server location
options.C: The gradual degradation in model performance as real-world data patterns diverge from training data
options.D: The model switching its output language based on changing user demographics

questions.question: What is the smartest first approach for an MSME to adopt AI with minimal upfront investment?

options.A: Start with AI-powered SaaS tools and APIs that solve specific problems, then scale based on results
options.B: Immediately purchase an enterprise-level AI licence for all departments
options.C: Wait until AI technology costs come down significantly before making any adoption decisions
options.D: Build a fully custom AI model in-house with a dedicated internal data science team

questions.question: In generative AI, what does the 'temperature' parameter control?

options.A: The number of output tokens the model generates per second
options.B: The physical heat generated by the GPU during intensive AI processing
options.C: The randomness or creativity of the model's output — higher values produce more varied responses
options.D: The speed at which the model generates and streams its responses

questions.question: What is the primary risk of deploying a biased AI model in a corporate hiring process?

options.A: The model will systematically discriminate against candidates based on protected characteristics like race or gender
options.B: The model will process job applications too slowly to be practical
options.C: The model will only select candidates who demand the highest salaries
options.D: The model will automatically reject all applications without any human review

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes 'zero-shot learning' in AI?

options.A: A model's ability to perform a task it was never explicitly trained on by leveraging general knowledge
options.B: A model that scores zero percent accuracy on any completely new task
options.C: Learning that is performed without requiring any computational processing resources
options.D: Training an AI model using zero data points from scratch

questions.question: For AI-driven digital marketing, which KPI is most important when evaluating an AI personalization engine?

options.A: The number of AI API calls made per day by the engine
options.B: Conversion rate lift and improvement in customer lifetime value
options.C: The social media following and public reputation of the AI vendor
options.D: The size and complexity of the underlying AI model used

questions.question: What is a 'knowledge graph' in the context of AI systems?

options.A: A visual performance chart showing an AI model's training accuracy over time
options.B: A structured representation of entities and their real-world relationships used to enhance AI reasoning
options.C: A network diagram showing the physical connections between AI hardware components
options.D: A graph tracking the learning progress and scores of individual students in an LMS

questions.question: Which component is most critical for a successful large-scale AI implementation in an enterprise?

options.A: Deploying the most computationally advanced and latest AI model available
options.B: High-quality, well-governed, and domain-relevant data
options.C: Allocating the largest possible IT infrastructure budget for the project
options.D: Rolling out AI tools across all departments simultaneously for maximum impact

questions.question: What does 'RLHF' stand for and why is it significant in AI development?

options.A: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback; used to align AI model behavior with human values and preferences
options.B: Real-time Language Handling Framework; used to process multilingual user queries
options.C: Randomized Learning for Human Features; used to generate realistic synthetic training data
options.D: Recursive Loop for Human Feedback; used primarily to speed up deep learning training cycles

questions.question: What is the main advantage of using cloud-based AI services over building on-premise AI infrastructure?

options.A: Cloud AI offers scalability, lower upfront costs, and access to state-of-the-art models without managing hardware
options.B: Cloud AI provides absolute 100% data privacy and regulatory compliance guarantees
options.C: Cloud AI completely eliminates the need for any internal AI knowledge or expertise
options.D: Cloud AI services always produce significantly more accurate results than local models

questions.question: Which statement best describes the future role of AI in India's workforce based on emerging global trends?

options.A: AI will completely eliminate all job categories across India within the next five years
options.B: AI will have minimal long-term impact on India due to the country's large manual and agrarian workforce
options.C: AI will only significantly affect white-collar IT sector jobs and leave other sectors untouched
options.D: AI will augment most roles while creating new job categories, making AI literacy and reskilling urgently necessary
